Video.summary: C.A. joins Rhett Morris in Charlotte Harbor for an inshore slam adventure in one of Florida’s most productive estuaries.
Video.title: "Harbor Master"

Video.summary: CA and Trevor field test some new Flats Class Exclusive Rods.
Video.title: "Two-Timing Trevor"

Video.summary: CA heads to the northern regions of the Nature Coast for a multi-species day.
Video.title: "Pressure Makes Diamonds"

Video.summary: Captains Jonathan Moss and Josh Greer are fishing for south Florida’s exotics amongst the alligators of the Florida Everglades.
Video.title: "Alligator Alley"

Video.summary: Jared's teams up with long time friend and fellow guide, Capt. Greg Dini, to conquer the patch reef.
Video.title: "Mutton Mania"

Video.summary: Guadalajara’s heritage runs deep. On the DIRT crew’s latest adventure, they explored the region’s rich history from volcanic highlands to secret surf coves. Chefs Xrysw Ruelas and Óscar Segundo sent Josh and co. to load up on locally grown coffee, spearfish between catching waves, and follow freshly harvested agave on its journey to becoming tequila. After learning the ropes of Mexico’s national sport, Charreria, and hitting up a Lucha Libre match, all the local flavors came together in a feast backed by thousands of years of tradition. There really is something special in the soil here.
Video.title: DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"
DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"
DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible out to the swordfish grounds.
Video.title: "Swordfishing out of Plantation Boat Mart"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger break in their new Invincible Catamaran with a trip to fish out of Palm Beach with their friend Captain Dave Fields.
Video.title: "Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"

Image.alt: "Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"
"Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"
"Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fly kites with Captain Dave Fields for a great day of sailfishing out of Sailfish Marina in Palm Beach.
Video.title: "Palm Beach Sailfishing"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take a long run out into the Gulf from Key West to sample the bottom fishing and the long range capability of their new Invincible Catamaran.
Video.title: "Way Out in the Gulf Part 1"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger continue fishing after taking a long run out into the Gulf from Key West to sample the bottom fishing and the long range capability of their new Invincible Catamaran.
Video.title: "Way Out in the Gulf Part 2"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fish out of Key West for tunas and sailfish while battling big seas and big sharks.
Video.title: "Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"

Image.alt: "Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"
"Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"
"Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"

Video.summary: When the yellowtail at the islands refuse to play, it’s time to switch up the game plan. Ali and Rush pivot north of San Diego in search of hard-charging bluefin tuna during a multi-day mission aboard the 43’ custom ACI Catamaran "Killer Bee". Armed with the right crew, the right gear, and a never-quit mindset, the guys roll the dice on an overnight move—hoping to wake up to redemption and put a few quality bluefin on deck.
Video.title: "Killer Bee"

Video.summary: Returning to Venice, Louisiana for the first time in nearly a decade, Rush reconnects with longtime friends Eric and Monique Newman of Journey South Outfitters for an unforgettable inshore fishing adventure in the heart of the Mississippi River Delta. With reports of massive schools of redfish cruising the beaches, Monique (“Moe”) must decide whether to trust the intel and make a bold run down the coast—or rely on her own instincts built from years on the water. The mission is simple: put Rush on a true bull redfish and see if he has what it takes to earn a spot in her coveted “Over 40-Inch Club.” This trip highlights why Venice, Louisiana is considered one of the premier inshore fisheries in the country—and why having the right guide makes all the difference. Target Species: Bull Redfish. Location: Venice, Louisiana – Mississippi Delta. Outfitter: Journey South Outfitters.
Video.title: "Moe On Mission"

Video.summary: While releasing a defeated tarpon healthy after an epic battle is the ultimate victory, everything else is measured in heartbreak and degrees of failure. For Justin, putting Ethan on his first silver king requires patience and a tasty diversion by a local chef.
Video.title: "Silver King Thing"

Video.summary: Meredith learns the value of the Trash Can Slam on a flounder trip with - Capt. Justin that turns into a multi-species challenge.”
Video.title: "Flounder To Slam"

Video.summary: Capt Justin Leake and Meredith McCord divert attention from what’s not happening with the tarpon bite to using CoastMaster, light tackle and experience to find snapper success on heavily fished public spots.
Video.title: "Dog Daze Delight"

Video.summary: When the Bay Point Billfish Open returns, we hop on board the Ellie B for some offshore competition and follow the event’s epic dockside party.
Video.title: "Billfish Open Rebirth"

Video.summary: Melissa Bachman has made her name over the last two decades as one of the most dedicated hunters and producers in the outdoor industry. In this episode we’ll tag along with Melissa while she chases big game around the world with her Winchester in hand. First we’ll head to Colorado as Melissa spot and stalks giant mule deer, then we’ll head across the pond to New Zealand to chase mountain ibex and tar, then head back to the states for Montana whitetail at ground level.
Video.title: “Big Game Bachman”

Video.summary: Shotgun sports are experiencing unprecedented growth, with competitions across the country gaining popularity as enthusiasts vie to be the best shooters in the world. Every summer, the Amateur Trapshooting Association hosts The Grand American in Sparta, Illinois. This year marks the 125th Anniversary for the largest trap shooting competition in the world. We’ll talk with the people who make this tournament run behind the scenes and explore what makes this tournament such a special event. We’ll also look at the rich history of The Grand. On-site we’ll chat with the curator of the Trapshooting Hall of Fame and look at some of the incredible historical pieces on display in the museum. Additionally, we’ll learn about White Flyer and how the clay manufacturer has made important innovations for the shotgun sports.
Video.title: "A Grand History"

Video.summary: Central Florida’s St. John’s River is home to countless species of wildlife, making this sweet water estuary the perfect playground for a pair of river rats, Captains Jonathan Moss and Nick Clark.
Video.title: "River Rats"

Video.summary: Locate and catch more suspended crappies during mid-winter conditions.
Video.title: "Suspended Crappies"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor launch out of Key Largo and fish their way up a creek filled with monsters.
Video.title: "Creek Monsters"

Video.summary: "On this episode of Huckberry Homes, we caught our good friend and photographer James Barkman out in the wild. He gave us a tour of his vintage ""Yellow Submarine"" inspired VW Van and broke down some of the modifications he has made to make this adventure bus feel more like home.
Video.title: "How This Photographer Crafted His Vintage Adventure Van | Ep. 8 James Barkman"

Image.alt: "How This Photographer Crafted His Vintage Adventure Van | Ep. 8 James Barkman"
"How This Photographer Crafted His Vintage Adventure Van | Ep. 8 James Barkman"
"How This Photographer Crafted His Vintage Adventure Van | Ep. 8 James Barkman"

Video.summary: Get your FREE Knot Guide here! — tomrowlandpodcast.com/knotguide - IS THIS THE BEST KNOT TO TIE YOUR HOOK TO YOUR LINE?! - Introducing the UNKNOWN Knot, named because I found it on Instagram! Today I will show you how to tie it, and we will test its knot breaking strength. This knot is for tying line to your hook. Were you surprised by the Unknown Knot? - You can see how to tie this knot on my Youtube Channel - Tom Rowland Podcast! - Follow @Tom_Rowland_Podcast on Instagram and share your suggestions for naming this remarkable knot, which earns its place as a reliable tool in the host's fishing arsenal! - How2Tuesday is presented by Danco Pliers! - Danco sports is an industry leading manufacturer of pliers, knives and accessories for anglers worldwide. Since our start in 2006, we have been committed to providing affordable, high quality products and outstanding customer service to anglers and fishing leaders alike. Danco Pliers is located in Stuart, Florida where we wear flip flops to work year-round. - Check out Danco here — https://bit.ly/tomrowlandpodcast2
Video.title: "The UNKNOWN Knot - Tie it! Test it!! | H2T"

Image.alt: "The UNKNOWN Knot - Tie it! Test it!! | H2T"
"The UNKNOWN Knot - Tie it! Test it!! | H2T"
"The UNKNOWN Knot - Tie it! Test it!! | H2T"

Video.summary: Do you have any fitness goals that you've been wanting to accomplish? Do you want to run a marathon, lose 50 pounds, compete in a cross fit competition, or all of the above? - Well Tom has a three step hack to achieving even the hardest of physical goals! Join us as we uncover those secrets and crush this transition into the new year! - Get my favorite supplements to help you on your fitness journey. - Electrolytes - Drink LMNT | Zero Sugar Electrolytes - https://bit.ly/3OTvkAn - Vitamin - Micro Factor Pack | 1st Phorm - https://bit.ly/46JMJ4Y - Protein - Phormula-1 Post-Workout Recovery Protien - https://bit.ly/45PLEqV - Magnesium - Magnesium Powder | 1st Phorm - https://bit.ly/3rwpvQt - If you have questions or suggestions for the show you can text Tom at 1 305-930-7346 or email Tom through email: Podcast@saltwaterexperience.com - This podcast is presented by Black Rifle Coffee. THE BEST DAMN COFFEE YOU'VE EVER HAD, PERIOD. Your favorite light roast, medium roast, and dark roast coffees are roasted here in the USA by a veteran-led team of people who are mission-focused to make sure nothing but the best hits your mug. Use this link http://bit.ly/3zDIRUa and this code BLASTOFF25 for 25% off your first month!
Video.title: "Three Hacks to Your Fitness Journey!" | PF

Image.alt: "Three Hacks to Your Fitness Journey!" | PF
"Three Hacks to Your Fitness Journey!" | PF
"Three Hacks to Your Fitness Journey!" | PF

Video.summary: Nobody chases the waterfowl migration as hard as Shawn Stahl of Team Winchester. The Michigan native spends over half of his year running the highways and flyways searching for the x. In this episode, we’ll tag along with Shawn and his crew with their Winchester pumps in hand as they hunker down hiding in fields and timber in hopes of locked wings from ducks and geese.
Video.title: "Stahled Out"

Video.summary: Overview: East Alton, Illinois is the home of Winchester Ammunition and the manufacturing home of the shotshell plants that build and produce millions of rounds of ammo for hunters, sport shooters, military and law enforcement. Get an unprecedented inside look at the birth cycle of a shotshell inside the walls of Winchester. From hull to primer, shot to wad and packaging to shipping, watch how the most legendary and successful ammunition brand in the world comes to is born and built.
Video.title: "Building The Legend"

Video.summary: Charles Rodney’s relationship with his hunting dogs, passion for field-to-table living and connection to the land make his love for chasing rabbits contagious while the heritage of Winchester’s small game ammunition makes this beloved pastime accessible for generations to come. Also: a Model 42 collection for the ages and hunting Kansas coyotes with the Keefer Brothers.
Video.title: "The Rabbit Hunter"

Video.summary: Deer season — the sights, the sounds and the smells stick with you. It’s about remembering those who came before us and setting the right example for those who look up to us. It’s about protecting the tradition, so they always have something to look forward to. When communities come together, we see the real reason for this special time of year.
Video.title: “Deer Season”

Video.summary: On opening day of Missouri’s gun season, hunters from around the state seek ridges and box blinds throughout the “Show Me” state in search of mature whitetail bucks. For Nikki Boxler and the Winchester Deer Camp, it’s no different. With Winchester’s in hand, Nikki and crew play cat and mouse with several mature bucks at Indian Hill Outfitters in Pike County.
Video.title: "Midwest Monarchs"

Video.summary: Every season, just like clockwork, mission-driven longboards gobble their way across the plains of South Dakota, the cornfields of Iowa, the timbered ridges of Michigan and the cattle ground of Kansas looking for their soulmates in one of the most celebrated hunting seasons in America…the wild turkey breeding season. Join Team Winchester as they stalk, call, flank and roost boss gobblers into range from coast to coast.
Video.title: “Lovesick Longbeards”

Video.summary: Capt. Jared travels to Venice, Louisiana, where the Mississippi River meets the Gulf, joining Capt. Garrett George and Capt. Jesse Leboeuf for an action-packed offshore adventure in pursuit of Redfish and Tuna.
Video.title: "LA Tuna Pt 2"

Video.summary: In the Florida Keys, legendary Capt. Paul Ross, son Capt. Pauly, and first mate Mason team up with Capt. Jared Raskob, where one Grouper drop leads to the unexpected.
Video.title: "Unexpected Guest"

Video.summary: What starts as a normal trip turns wild as Capt. Jared and Capt. Garrett get swarmed by Cobia.
Video.title: "The Prodigal Son"

Video.summary: The sharks won’t stop Capt. Jared and special Guest Capt. Jimmy Hendricks from having their day.
Video.title: "Variety Pack"

Video.summary: In this episode the DCD crew heads to the lesser-known but highly productive Red River in Western Louisiana. Despite a slow start to the season, the Red River provides some of the best hunting yet. With mixed bags of woodies, greenheads, and teal. Not to mention a handful of bonus squirrels and wild mushrooms. Shoot straight and come hungry.
Video.title: "The Red River Provides"

Video.summary: Duck hunting in Louisiana is as much about its history as it is about its future. In the season's final days, Jean-Paul learns about Louisiana’s rich waterfowling history, dives into conservation projects throughout the state, and returns to Bayou Black for one last ride. Shoot straight and come hungry because it's the last hunt of the season.
Video.title: "Gravies, Gogs & Pirogues"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve start the season with Capt. Scott Stoky chasing Islamorada Tunas and Mahis offshore out of Plantation Boat Mart.
Video.title: "Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"

Image.alt: "Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"
"Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"
"Islamorada Blackfins And Mahis With Capt Scott Stoky"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve catch a mixed bag bottom fishing off of Islamorada with Captain Scott Stoky.
Video.title: "Islamorada Mixed Bag"

Video.summary: We head to Northern MN in search of the perfect family getaway!
Video.title: "Mantrap Memories"

Video.summary: Cliff Lake in NW Ontario is home to some amazing muskie fishing, and we take a crack at them!
Video.title: "Muskies at Cliff"

Video.summary: Knobby's Fly-In and Outpost does it again - unreal fishing!
Video.title: "Devil’s Rapids Walleyes"

Video.summary: Early Season Smallmouth are a blast and learning how to locate them is the key to success!
Video.title: "Soft Water Smallies"

Video.summary: Today, Capt. Danny is with his good friend Frank Gidus. The two are speaking at the “Local Waters” social this evening, so why not fish before the event in Frank’s local waters…
Video.title: "Local Waters"

Video.summary: In this episode Captain Brandon Branch takes one of his good buddies from Diamond Fishing Products out for some Shallow Grouper action over in Crystal River Florida. Year after year this style of fishing is becoming more popular and provides trophy quality fish in less than 10ft of water
Video.title: "Shallow Water Studs"

Video.summary: In this episode of Local Lines Captain Brandon Branch will be fishing with his tournament partner Captain Brian Sawyer. The goal from this trip is to show everyone what the nature coast has to offer 12 months out of the year!
Video.title: "Backcountry Magic On The Nature Coast"

Video.summary: In this episode Captain Brandon Branch decided to go back to his roots and fish with a long time friend Laremy Strickland. They've been fishing together for over 10 years now and on this episode they'll be competing to see who can catch the biggest bag.
Video.title: "How It All Started"

Video.summary: Captain Brand Branch ends season one of Local Lines with a bang as he fishes with his good friend and Cedar Key Legend Captain Danny Allen of Southern Salt Charters. This may be the last episode of the season but it's just the beginning of an amazing journey.
Video.title: "The Finale"

Video.summary: It disappeared for generations and seasons on end, only to return again. This time, rivaling the golden era of the past. Explore the history of the beginning of sportfishing and how it has influenced the most prestigious anglers in Southern California.
Video.title: "Folklore: Year of the Bluefin"
